temporal_description:
(00:00-00:03) In a dimly lit, dark room, two men in suits sit at a table. The man on the left, who has a mustache, takes a drag from a cigarette and then uses a long metal tool to ash it into a small, green ceramic ashtray on the table. The man on the right, who wears glasses, watches him intently. (Storyline: The scene establishes a tense and clandestine meeting between two men. The act of ashing the cigarette is a classic noir trope, suggesting a moment of decision or a pause before a significant event.) (Audio: 1) Tone of speech: Serious, low. 2) Background sounds or music: None.)
(00:04-00:07) The man with the mustache looks up, his face sweaty and his expression grim. He listens as the man with glasses continues to speak, informing him that 'Mao Ge' sent him to deliver a message. (Storyline: The plot is advanced as it's revealed that the man with glasses is delivering a message from a third party, 'Mao Ge,' to the man with the mustache. This introduces a new layer of intrigue.) (Audio: 1) Tone of speech: Serious, informative. 2) Background sounds or music: None.)
